name: Saffron
summary: Saffron is a stigma monograph with mood-support and sensory-positioning relevance. It is high-value for the system because it has frontend appeal, a relatively distinct chemistry, and practical differentiation from generic calming herbs.
description: Saffron is used as culinary stigma threads, powders, and standardized extracts. Key compounds include crocin, safranal, and picrocrocin. Position around mood/stress support with restrained claims and careful cost/quality framing because adulteration risk is meaningful.
mechanism: Proposed mechanism includes serotonergic and glutamatergic modulation together with antioxidant and neuroprotective effects associated with crocins and volatile constituents such as safranal.
safetyNotes: Usually tolerated in supplement-range doses, but dizziness, GI upset, headache, or overstimulation are possible in some users.
preparation: Whole stigma, powders, capsules, and standardized extracts.
region: Cultivated in Southwest Asia, the Mediterranean, and other dry temperate regions.
